title: Speedboat Hire in Puerto Banus: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus, the glamorous marina on the Costa del Sol, is the perfect starting point for speedboat hire. Depart from the main quay or nearby Marbella and expect to pay from EUR 300 for a half-day self-drive rental. Compare verified listings on BoatHire24 to secure the best deal.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus?

Half-day self-drive speedboat hire in Puerto Banus typically costs between EUR 300 and EUR 600. Prices vary by boat size, engine power, and season. A shared tour on a RIB might cost from EUR 80 per person, while a private luxury speedboat can exceed EUR 1,500 for a full day. Always check the total including fuel and insurance on boat rental Puerto Banus listings.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most speedboat rentals depart from Puerto Banus marina itself, adjacent to the famous luxury boutiques. Alternatively, you can launch from Marbella's Real Club Náutico, just 5 km east. For a quieter start, consider Cabopino harbour to the west, or head to Benalmádena Marina if you prefer a larger base. The nearby islands of Isla de las Palomas and Isla Canela are reachable on a longer cruise.

Which boat to rent

Choose the right speedboat for your group and purpose.

  1. Olympic 490, Classic open bowrider for couples; up to 4 guests; from EUR 350 half-day. See the Olympic 490 listing for details.
  2. QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, Spacious day cruiser with 150 HP; seats 6; from EUR 450 half-day.
  3. Axopar 28, Modern crossover with cabin; for 8 guests; from EUR 700 half-day.
  4. Sunseeker 31 Superhawk, High-performance luxury; for 6 guests; from EUR 1,200 half-day.

Costs at a glance

General price bands for Puerto Banus boat hire.

  • Shared RIB tour: from EUR 80 per person for 2 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 guests): EUR 300, 600
  • Catamaran full-day (up to 12 guests): EUR 1,200, 2,500
  • Crewed yacht full-day (10 guests): EUR 2,000, 4,000

Do you need a licence to hire a speedboat in Puerto Banus?

Yes, a valid boating licence is required for self-drive hire unless you choose a boat with an instructor. For Spanish waters, a recreational licence (PER or PNB) is typically needed, but many operators accept an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). Always confirm exact requirements with the operator as rules may vary. BoatHire24 listings clearly state licence needs for each boat.

Tip: Book early in high season (June to September) and brace for the Levante wind, which can stiffen afternoon sea breezes in the Strait of Gibraltar area. A morning departure often gives calmer conditions.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 300, 2,500+ per day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Max capacity: 4, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Required for self-drive (ICC or Spanish)

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a speedboat in Puerto Banus without a licence?

Yes, some operators offer guided tours where a skipper drives the boat, so no licence is needed.

What is the best time of year for speedboat hire in Puerto Banus?

The best time is from May to October, with July and August offering the warmest weather and calmest seas.

How far can I travel in a half-day rental from Puerto Banus?

With a half-day speedboat you can reach Marbella to the east or Cabopino to the west, covering about 20 nautical miles round trip.

Puerto Banus remains a premier destination for speedboat hire in 2026. Whether you prefer self-drive or a skipper, BoatHire24 connects you with reliable operators. Boat owners can list your boat on the platform and reach thousands of travellers daily.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
